value:
Thanks for reaching out! I'd be happy to help you set up a default page for your Azure WebApp.

To serve a default page, you'll want to ensure your default document is properly configured in Azure. The most common approach is to name your main file `index.html` or `default.html` and place it in the root directory of your site (the `wwwroot` folder). Azure will automatically serve these as your default page when someone visits your domain without specifying a file.

If you've already uploaded your HTML files via FTP, double-check that your main page file is named one of those standard default names and is in the root directory. If you're using a different filename, you may need to configure it explicitly through the Azure portal under your WebApp's Application settings or web.config file.

Could you let me know what filename you're currently using for your default page? That way I can give you more specific guidance if the standard approach isn't working for you.
